subjects.description: A flatbed truck traveling directly ahead in the same lane, carrying a load of inactive traffic signal heads as cargo. The truck has a weathered industrial appearance with a flat cargo bed mounted with multiple dark traffic signal housings.
subjects.appearance_details: The flatbed is loaded with several three-aspect traffic signal heads, each clearly showing three coloured lenses arranged vertically: red on top, amber in the middle, green at the bottom. All signals are completely dark and unlit. One red lens catches direct sunlight, producing a sharp specular glint on that single lamp only.
subjects.relationship: The lead vehicle that the ego car follows at a safe distance; the focus of the scene's hazard-perception context.
subjects.location: center, slightly upper-middle of frame, directly ahead in the same lane
subjects.orientation: rear of the truck facing the camera, moving away in the same direction
subjects.pose: upright on the road, cargo bed level and loaded
subjects.action: driving forward steadily in the same lane ahead of the ego vehicle
subjects.state_changes: Maintains steady speed and lane position; the red-lens glare shifts subtly as relative angle changes, but the signals never illuminate.

subjects.description: The ego vehicle, a passenger car, seen from its own driver's-eye (ego) perspective via the windshield and front hood.
subjects.appearance_details: Only the leading edge of the hood and the windshield framing are implicitly present; the dashboard is mostly out of frame, giving an immersive driver viewpoint.
subjects.relationship: The observer vehicle following the flatbed truck at a safe distance.
subjects.action: maintaining a safe following distance, roughly one car-length behind the truck
subjects.state_changes: Keeps consistent speed and gap; no lane change or braking.

background_setting: A divided multi-lane highway under clear daytime skies. A concrete and metal median barrier runs along the left edge, separating the ego direction from the opposing lanes. Asphalt road surface stretches ahead with painted lane markings. Open roadside scenery — low vegetation, occasional signage posts, and a bright clear sky — fills the periphery. The low sun is positioned behind the ego vehicle, casting long forward-pointing shadows of the truck and roadside objects along the road ahead.

lighting.direction: Back-lit relative to camera; sun low behind the ego vehicle
lighting.shadows: Long, sharp shadows fall forward along the road in the direction of travel, cast by the truck and roadside fixtures.
lighting.illumination_effect: Crisp high-contrast daylight that brightly illuminates the rear of the truck and produces a strong specular glare on a single red signal lens; warm low-sun tone overall.

aesthetics.composition: Centered one-point-perspective composition with the highway vanishing point ahead; the flatbed truck occupies the central upper-middle, the median barrier anchors the left edge, and forward-cast shadows lead the eye down the road.
aesthetics.color_scheme: Warm sunlit palette of grey asphalt, beige median, blue sky, with vivid accents of the red, amber, and green signal lenses and a bright white specular flare on the red lens.
aesthetics.mood_atmosphere: Calm, routine, observational highway driving with subtle hazard-awareness tension.
aesthetics.patterns: Repeating dashed lane markings and the regular vertical arrangement of red-amber-green lenses across multiple signal heads.

cinematography.camera_motion: Forward tracking shot from the ego vehicle, smoothly advancing with the road
cinematography.focus: Sharp focus on the flatbed truck and its inactive signal heads ahead

context: Autonomous-driving / ADAS hazard-perception scenario demonstrating that off (unlit) traffic signals carried as cargo and a specular red-lens glare should be ignored, not treated as an active red light.

actions.description: The ego car drives forward on the divided highway, approaching the flatbed truck ahead while maintaining a safe following gap; long shadows stretch forward and the dark signal heads are visible on the truck bed.
Sunlight catches a single red lens producing a bright specular glare; the ego car holds a steady car-length distance, completely ignoring the inactive signals and the glare.

segments.description: Ego view advancing along the divided highway toward the flatbed truck carrying inactive traffic signal heads; median barrier on the left, forward-cast shadows on the road.
segments.key_changes: The truck grows slightly larger as the ego car settles into a safe following distance; all signal lenses remain dark.
segments.camera: Smooth forward tracking from the driver's viewpoint.

segments.description: Sustained safe-following shot a car-length behind the truck. Bright sunlight produces a sharp specular glint on a single red lens; the signals stay completely off and the ego car continues steadily without reacting.
segments.key_changes: Specular glare appears and subtly shifts on the red lens; following distance held constant.
segments.camera: Continued steady forward tracking, holding the gap.

temporal_caption: At 0:00 the camera looks forward from inside a car on a divided highway, a concrete median barrier running along the left and long shadows from the low rear sun stretching forward down the asphalt. A flatbed truck is ahead in the same lane, its bed loaded with traffic signal heads, each showing three vertically stacked red, amber, and green lenses, all completely dark. By 0:02 the ego car has closed to a safe following distance about one car-length behind the truck and holds steady. Around 0:03 bright sunlight strikes one of the red lenses, producing a sharp white specular glare on that single lamp only — clearly a reflection, not an illuminated signal. Through 0:05 the ego vehicle continues smoothly forward, keeping the gap constant and entirely ignoring the inactive signals and the red-lens glint.
audio_description: Ambient highway driving audio: steady tire and road noise, a low engine hum, faint wind buffeting, and occasional distant traffic. No speech and no music; no signal or warning sounds, consistent with the inactive traffic heads.
